seo.title: Caching Strategies implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Caching Strategies projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for deploying and maintaining high-performance caching layers. It covers distributed storage configuration, AI response optimization, invalidation patterns, and edge delivery to ensure system reliability and cost efficiency.

items.title: Configure Maxmemory Eviction Policy
items.description: Set the maxmemory-policy to 'allkeys-lru' or 'volatile-lru' in Redis to ensure the system gracefully handles memory saturation without crashing.

items.title: Implement Connection Pooling
items.description: Verify that the application client uses a persistent connection pool to eliminate the latency overhead of TCP handshakes on every request.

items.title: Define Memory Limits for Fragmentation
items.description: Limit cache memory usage to 75% of available system RAM to provide overhead for fragmentation and replication buffers.

items.title: Optimize Serialization Formats
items.description: Replace JSON serialization with binary formats like MessagePack or Protobuf for large objects to reduce memory footprint and CPU overhead.

items.title: Disable Unnecessary Persistence
items.description: Turn off RDB and AOF persistence if the cache is purely transient to reduce disk I/O and improve write throughput.

items.title: Standardize Prompt Normalization
items.description: Trim whitespace, convert to lowercase, and sort parameters in prompt strings before hashing to increase cache hit rates for identical queries.

items.title: Implement Semantic Key Hashing
items.description: Use vector embeddings and similarity thresholds to identify and serve cached responses for semantically similar LLM prompts.

items.title: Strip Non-Deterministic Metadata
items.description: Remove unique identifiers, timestamps, and usage tokens from LLM provider responses before storing them in the cache.

items.title: Tiered TTLs by Task Type
items.description: Assign 1-hour TTLs to creative tasks and 24-hour+ TTLs to factual extraction tasks to balance freshness and cost.

items.title: Cache Token Usage Metrics
items.description: Store the token count of cached responses to accurately report cost savings and monitor API quota utilization.

items.title: Implement Cache Stampede Protection
items.description: Use a mutex or 'singleflight' pattern to ensure only one upstream request is made when a high-traffic key expires.

items.title: Deploy Tag-Based Invalidation
items.description: Group related keys using sets or cache tags to allow for bulk purging of specific data categories (e.g., all products in a category).

items.title: Configure Stale-While-Revalidate
items.description: Set up the client to serve the expired cache entry while asynchronously fetching fresh data from the origin.

items.title: Validate Atomic Updates
items.description: Use Lua scripts or multi-key transactions to ensure that related cache keys are updated or deleted simultaneously.

items.title: Set Default TTLs for All Keys
items.description: Enforce a global default TTL on every 'SET' operation to prevent 'zombie' data from consuming memory indefinitely.

items.title: Verify Cache-Control Directives
items.description: Ensure 's-maxage' is configured for shared CDN caching and 'max-age' for private browser caching.

items.title: Restrict Vary Headers
items.description: Limit the 'Vary' header to 'Accept-Encoding' to prevent the CDN from creating unique cache entries for every User-Agent string.

items.title: Configure Origin Shielding
items.description: Enable a regional cache layer between the edge nodes and the origin to collapse redundant requests across different geographic regions.

items.title: Define Cookie Bypass Rules
items.description: Explicitly list session and authentication cookies that should trigger a cache bypass to prevent serving private data to other users.

items.title: Implement Purge API Authentication
items.description: Secure the edge cache purge endpoints with API keys or IP allowlists to prevent unauthorized cache clearing.

items.title: Monitor Cache Hit Rate (CHR)
items.description: Set up real-time dashboards and alerts for when the CHR falls below a defined baseline (e.g., < 80% for static assets).

items.title: Track P99 Cache Latency
items.description: Monitor the 99th percentile latency of cache lookups to detect network congestion or expensive key serialization.

items.title: Audit Large Keys
items.description: Run periodic scans for keys exceeding 512KB, as these can block the event loop in single-threaded caches like Redis.

items.title: Test Cache-Down Fallback
items.description: Verify that the application gracefully degrades and fetches directly from the database if the cache cluster is unreachable.

items.title: Log Eviction Rates
items.description: Monitor the 'evicted_keys' metric; an increasing rate indicates the cache size is too small for the working set.

items.title: Enforce Tenant Key Isolation
items.description: Prefix all cache keys with a unique tenant or user ID to prevent cross-account data leakage in multi-tenant systems.

items.title: Verify PII Stripping
items.description: Audit cache values to ensure no unencrypted Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is stored in the caching layer.

items.title: Enable TLS in Transit
items.description: Configure the application to connect to the cache using TLS 1.2+ to protect data against packet sniffing in the internal network.

items.title: Rotate Cache Access Credentials
items.description: Implement a policy for rotating Redis or CDN API keys every 90 days without causing downtime.

items.title: Disable Remote Command Execution
items.description: Rename or disable dangerous commands like 'FLUSHALL' or 'CONFIG' in production cache environments.
